title: armeria arenaria subsp. marginata
intro: Armeria arenaria subsp. marginata, also known as the Sea Thrift or Cystopteris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae, characterized by its pink or white flowers and silvery-green leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Armeria arenaria subsp. marginata include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
